title: DoorDash Q1 2026: Revenue Misses, But Profit Beat and Strong Guidance Fuel Rally
text: DoorDash reported mixed Q1 2026 results that ultimately pleased investors with a strong stock rally. The company posted revenue of $4.04 billion versus analyst estimates of $4.14 billion, representing a 2.8% revenue miss. However, on the earnings front, GAAP profit of $0.42 per share was 15.4% above analyst consensus estimates. Adjusted EBITDA was $754 million versus analyst estimates of $741.5 million, representing a 1.7% beat. DoorDash's GOV rose 37% from a year ago to $31.6 billion and beat a $31.5 billion estimate from analysts. The stock pop reflected investor focus on operational efficiency and strong demand indicators despite the revenue shortfall. The company reported record membership signups with DashPass growth accelerating, driving higher order frequency and customer stickiness. Total Orders increased 27% year-over-year to 933 million, demonstrating robust underlying demand across all business segments. Gross margin came in at 51.9%, ahead of analyst estimates of 51.6%, showing continued operational leverage. The company's guidance for Q2 expecting marketplace GOV of $32.4-$33.4 billion significantly exceeded analyst consensus of $31.8 billion, signaling management confidence in continued strong demand.
